allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Initial commit: Oja project with complete BMAD planning artifacts

- Product Brief: Budget-first shopping confidence app
- PRD: 190 FRs, 59 NFRs across all domains
- Architecture: Next.js 16, PWA, Supabase, offline-first
- UX Design: Complete specification with design system
- Epics: 10 epics, 107 stories with Given/When/Then ACs
- BMAD framework: Agents, workflows, templates

Tech Stack: Next.js 16 \(App Router\), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS,
Supabase \(Auth/DB/Realtime\), PWA \(Serwist\), Zustand, TanStack Query

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
Bash(git -C "C:/Users/diloc/Documents/programmer/oja" add .easignore .npmrc eas.json package.json package-lock.json app.json .mcp.json)

Bash(git -C "C:/Users/diloc/Documents/programmer/oja" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix: Add EAS build config and fix dependency resolution

- Add .easignore to exclude dev artifacts from cloud builds \(83MB → 3MB\)
- Add .npmrc with legacy-peer-deps to fix Clerk/React peer conflict
- Add eas.json with development/preview/production profiles
- Add expo-dev-client for dev builds with native modules
- Add Expo MCP server config to .mcp.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(AsyncStorage is null".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(bash .claude/hooks/bmad-speak.sh "Winston" "Diloc, preparing for production launch - this is where careful architecture pays dividends. Looking at your Oja app, I see a solid foundation with Expo SDK 54, Convex backend, Clerk auth, and Stripe payments. But let me speak plainly about what needs scrutiny before you open the gates. Critical Architecture Concerns: Security Posture - Your require current user pattern in Convex is good, but I''m concerned about the admin impersonation feature. That''s powerful and dangerous. Need to verify audit logging is immutable and comprehensive before production. Stripe Integration - Your documentation notes subscription system has core bugs fixed but needs end-to-end live testing. That''s a red flag for production. You cannot go live with untested payment flows. Period. Data Retention - Your cron jobs for price history compression and receipt cleanup are smart, but have you tested the edge cases? What happens if a cron fails mid-execution? Do you have idempotency guarantees? Rate Limiting - I don''t see any mention of rate limiting on your Convex mutations. With 30 voice assistant function tools and receipt scanning, you''re exposed to abuse. Need to implement rate limiting before launch. The foundation is solid, but we need to pressure-test these critical paths. What''s your timeline for launch?")

Bash(bash .claude/hooks/bmad-speak.sh "Murat" "Winston''s hitting the architecture angle hard - good. Let me give you the quality perspective, and I''m going to be blunt because this is production we''re talking about. Testing Coverage Reality Check: Your test suite shows 42 Jest unit tests and 16 Playwright E2E specs. That sounds decent on paper, but let''s talk about risk-based coverage. Critical Paths Under-Tested: Stripe webhook handling, Receipt processing pipeline, Convex cron jobs, Admin impersonation audit trail. Test Quality Issues I''d Investigate: Your E2E tests use click pressable helper because animated pressable doesn''t work with normal clicks - that''s a test architecture smell. Network idle never fires due to Convex WebSocket. Test data factories edge cases. Before Launch - Non-Negotiable: Load testing on Convex queries with real data volumes, Chaos testing on payment flows, Security testing on admin endpoints, Accessibility audit. You''ve got 42 unit tests - but do they test what actually breaks in production? That''s the question.")

Bash(for f in components/AddItemForm.tsx components/AddItemsModal.tsx components/UnmatchedItemsModal.tsx components/TripSummaryModal.tsx components/HealthAnalysisModal.tsx components/VariantPicker.tsx components/ListCard.tsx hooks/useVoiceAssistant.ts hooks/useProductScanner.ts)

Bash(for file in convex/stripe/credits.ts convex/stripe/checkout.ts convex/points/helpers.ts convex/listItems/pricing.ts convex/partners/helpers.ts convex/partners/messages.ts convex/partners/comments.ts convex/partners/members.ts convex/users/accountManagement.ts convex/pricingConfig.ts convex/listItems/core.ts convex/pantry/restock.ts)
Bash(for file in convex/insights/monthlyTrends.ts convex/insights/personalBests.ts convex/insights/streaks.ts convex/insights/helpers.ts convex/insights/challenges.ts)

Bash(do sed -i 's/\(q: any\)/q/g; s/\(item: any\)/item/g; s/\(r: any\)/r/g; s/\(s: any\)/s/g; s/\(a: any, b: any\)/\(a, b\)/g; s/\(a: any\)/a/g; s/\(i: any\)/i/g; s/\(p: any\)/p/g; s/\(u: any\)/u/g; s/\(c: any\)/c/g; s/: any\)/\)\\/g; s/handler: async \(ctx: any, args: any\)/handler: async \(ctx, args\)/g' "$file")

Bash(do sed -i 's/\(q: any\)/q/g; s/\(item: any\)/item/g; s/\(r: any\)/r/g; s/\(s: any\)/s/g; s/\(a: any, b: any\)/\(a, b\)/g; s/\(a: any\)/a/g; s/\(i: any\)/i/g; s/\(p: any\)/p/g; s/\(u: any\)/u/g; s/\(c: any\)/c/g' "$file")
